name: Flight-Test Build-Up Approach
summary: A safety-first way of expanding a test envelope by moving from low-risk conditions to higher-risk conditions with clear stop criteria.
whenToUseIt: Use it for flight-test planning whenever an aircraft is new, modified, or being pushed toward an unexplored corner of the envelope.
steps: Start with the lowest-risk configuration and condition set.
Define the exact test points, hazards, and abort criteria.
Fly the first points and compare measured response to prediction.
Review the data before authorising the next increment.
Expand gradually until the full envelope objective is reached.
example: Instead of jumping straight to high-angle-of-attack handling tests, a flight-test team begins at benign speeds and centre-of-gravity positions, then expands one axis at a time only after the previous data shows expected behaviour.
